verification.target_vocab_notes: 果物 appears as subject in 果物が食べたい
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: な-adjective 新鮮, が (subject), たい (want to), んだ (explanatory). All acceptable N5 patterns
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: たい desire + んだ. Vocabulary: N5 (新鮮、食べる). Length: Short. Context: Food desire
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with たい form and explanatory んだ. Natural expression

verification.target_vocab_notes: 止まる appears in negative form 止まりません (doesn't stop)
verification.kana_notes: Shows どまりません but should be とまりません. The と reading is correct, not ど
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: の (possessive), が (subject), ません (negative polite). Simple negative statement
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple negative. Vocabulary: N5 (トイレ、水). Length: Short. Context: Plumbing problem
verification.overall_notes: MAJOR kana error: どまりません should be とまりません. Critical reading error

verification.target_vocab_notes: 止まる/停まる appears in dictionary form まで clause
verification.grammar_notes: Contains まで (until) clause which creates a time-conditional construction that's more N4 territory. The 'V-るまで' pattern is often considered N4+
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: まで temporal (borderline N4). Vocabulary: N5 (電車、開ける). Length: Medium. Context: Safety instruction
verification.overall_notes: まで with verb (止まるまで) creates temporal condition that's N4+. Also uses ないで negative request

verification.target_vocab_notes: 止まる appears in ように purpose clause
verification.grammar_notes: Contains ように (so that/in order that) which is a purpose/result construction generally considered N4+. The Vるように pattern is beyond basic N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: ように purpose clause (N4+). Vocabulary: N5 (バス、手、上げる). Length: Medium. Context: Signaling
verification.overall_notes: Contains N4+ ように purpose clause. Too advanced for N5

verification.target_vocab_notes: 着く appears in dictionary form 着く
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: が (subject), 先に (first), へ (direction), dictionary form, よ (particle). All basic N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple future/present. Vocabulary: N5 (先、そこ). Length: Short. Context: Arrival prediction
verification.overall_notes: Clean N5 example. Natural use of 着く with directional へ

verification.target_vocab_notes: 着く appears in たら conditional (着いたら)
verification.grammar_notes: Contains たら conditional form (着いたら - when/if you arrive) which is explicitly listed as N4+ in guidelines. Conditionals with たら are not allowed in N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: たら conditional (N4+). Vocabulary: N5 (あちら、手紙、ください). Length: Medium. Context: Request upon arrival
verification.overall_notes: Contains N4+ たら conditional. Should use simpler construction for N5

verification.target_vocab_notes: 着く appears in dictionary form
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: いつ (when), の (explanatory/nominalizer). Simple question pattern, all N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Very simple when-question. Vocabulary: Very basic. Length: Very short. Context: Arrival time
verification.overall_notes: Perfect simple N5 question. Clear and natural

verification.target_vocab_notes: つく appears meaning 'to follow' (ついてくる), not 'to arrive' (着く)
verification.grammar_notes: Contains てくる (ついてくる - follows/comes with). This is N4+ according to guidelines. Also uses different meaning of つく (follow, not arrive)
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: てくる directional (N4+). Vocabulary: Different verb meaning. Length: Long. Context: Pet following
verification.overall_notes: Double issue: N4+ てくる AND wrong meaning (follow vs arrive). Wrong example for 着く (arrive)

verification.target_vocab_notes: つく appears meaning 'arrived' in past tense
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: は (topic), と (quotation), 言った (said). Direct quotation is acceptable N5. The quoted statement uses basic past tense
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Direct quotation with と. Vocabulary: N5 (昨日、ここ、言う). Length: Long. Context: Reported speech
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example showing quotation with と. Natural use of 着く in quoted speech

verification.target_vocab_notes: つく appears meaning 'to take a seat' (に着く), related but slightly different from 'arrive'
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: は (topic), の (possessive), に (direction/destination), た (past). Simple past tense statement
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple past tense. Vocabulary: N5 (レストラン、テーブル). Length: Short. Context: Being seated
verification.overall_notes: Acceptable N5. テーブルにつく means 'take a seat at table', related to arrival concept

verification.target_vocab_notes: 大好き appears twice in になるか...になるか pattern
verification.grammar_notes: Contains か...か...どちらか pattern which is complex conditional/alternative structure. Also uses なる with な-adjective. The overall complexity pushes beyond basic N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Complex か...か alternative (borderline N4). Vocabulary: N5 (大嫌い). Length: Long. Context: Binary choice
verification.overall_notes: Too complex with か...か...どちらか structure. Also んですね adds complexity. Beyond basic N5

verification.target_vocab_notes: 大好き appears as predicate in が大好きです
verification.grammar_notes: Uses basic N5: は (topic), が (subject of like), です (copula). Standard preference pattern
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Very simple preference. Vocabulary: Very basic (犬). Length: Very short. Context: Liking animals
verification.overall_notes: Perfect simple N5 example. Clear demonstration of 大好き with standard が pattern

verification.target_vocab_notes: 大好き appears as predicate
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: に (frequency adverb with に), を (object), 開く (open/hold), の (nominalizer), が (subject), だ. While 頻繁に may be advanced vocabulary, grammar is N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Nominalization pattern. Vocabulary: 頻繁 may be beyond N5. Length: Medium. Context: Party hosting
verification.overall_notes: Grammar is acceptable N5 but 頻繁 (frequent) is likely too advanced vocabulary for N5 learners

verification.target_vocab_notes: 妹 appears in の possessive phrase 私の妹
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: は (topic), の (possessive), を (object), 撮る (take photo), た (past), です. All basic N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Relative clause modifying 写真. Vocabulary: N5 (写真、撮る). Length: Medium. Context: Photo description
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with relative clause. Natural use of 妹

verification.target_vocab_notes: 妹 appears with honorific さん (妹さん - someone else's sister)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: の (possessive), に (target), たい (want to), ものです (desire expression). All acceptable N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: たい + ものです. Vocabulary: N5 (会う). Length: Medium. Context: Desire to meet
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example. Shows proper use of さん for someone else's sister

verification.target_vocab_notes: 妹 appears with さん honorific
verification.kana_notes: Shows なんねんう but should be なんねんせい (what grade). Missing せい and has wrong う
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: の (possessive), は (topic), 何年生 (what grade), の (explanatory). Grammar is acceptable N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple question. Vocabulary: N5 (何年生). Length: Short. Context: School grade inquiry
verification.overall_notes: MAJOR kana error: なんねんう should be なんねんせい. Critical mistake in reading

verification.target_vocab_notes: 夏 appears as topic with は
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: い-adjective 涼しい, は (topic), 好き (like), です, か. Standard preference question
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple preference question. Vocabulary: N5 (涼しい、好き). Length: Short. Context: Season preference
verification.overall_notes: Clean N5 example. Natural preference question about summer

verification.target_vocab_notes: 夏 appears in この前の夏 (last summer)
verification.grammar_notes: Contains から...ている pattern (この前の夏から...住んでいます) expressing duration from past to present. This is N4+ territory. Also uses ている
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: から + ている duration (N4+). Vocabulary: N5 (一緒、住む). Length: Long. Context: Living together duration
verification.overall_notes: Contains N4+ から...ている duration pattern. Too advanced for N5

verification.target_vocab_notes: 夏 appears in ある夏の午後 (one summer afternoon)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: は (topic), ある (certain - な-adjective), の (possessive), に (time), 初めて (first time), た (past). All basic N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Time expression with に. Vocabulary: N5 (初めて、会う、午後). Length: Medium. Context: First meeting
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example. Natural time expression with 夏

verification.target_vocab_notes: 今晩 appears as time expression
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: では (then), また (again), ね (particle), さようなら. All basic N5 farewell expressions
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple farewell phrases. Vocabulary: Very basic greetings. Length: Short. Context: Parting
verification.overall_notes: Natural N5 farewell expression. Good use of 今晩 in context

verification.target_vocab_notes: 今晩 appears as time topic
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: な-adjective 暇, か (question), な (sentence-ending particle). Simple casual question
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple copula question. Vocabulary: N5 (暇). Length: Very short. Context: Availability
verification.overall_notes: Simple casual N5 question. Natural use of かな ending

verification.target_vocab_notes: 今晩 appears as time expression
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: が (subject), time expression, 来る (come), ます. Simple future/present statement
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Very simple present/future. Vocabulary: Very basic (友達、来る). Length: Very short. Context: Visitor announcement
verification.overall_notes: Perfect simple N5 example. Clear and natural

verification.target_vocab_notes: 塩 appears as object in 塩を入れる
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: を (object), 入れる (put in), ました (past polite), か (question). Simple past question
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple past tense question. Vocabulary: Very basic (入れる). Length: Very short. Context: Cooking
verification.overall_notes: Clean simple N5 question. Natural cooking context

verification.target_vocab_notes: 塩 appears as object
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: を (object), 取る (take/pass), てください (please request), ませんか (polite negative question). Standard polite request pattern
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Polite request with てくださいませんか. Vocabulary: N5 (取る). Length: Medium. Context: Table request
verification.overall_notes: Good polite N5 request. Natural table manner expression

verification.target_vocab_notes: 塩 appears with honorific お (お塩)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: お (honorific prefix), 取る (pass), て (te-form imperative). Casual imperative request with て is basic N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Casual て-form request. Vocabulary: Very basic (取る). Length: Very short. Context: Table request
verification.overall_notes: Simple casual N5 request. Shows te-form imperative naturally

verification.target_vocab_notes: 先週 appears in から phrase indicating starting time
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: は (topic), から (from/since), である (formal copula - N5). While である is formal, it's taught at N5. The から here shows duration which is acceptable N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: である formal copula with から. Vocabulary: N5 (病気). Length: Medium. Context: Illness duration
verification.overall_notes: Acceptable N5. である is formal but taught at N5. Natural duration expression

verification.target_vocab_notes: 先週 appears as time expression
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: に (indirect object/beneficiary), time expression, が (subject), 生まれる, た. All basic N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple past tense. Vocabulary: N5 (子供、生まれる). Length: Short. Context: Birth announcement
verification.overall_notes: Clean N5 example. Natural use of 先週 with birth verb

verification.target_vocab_notes: 先週 appears as time expression at sentence start
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: time expression, は (topic), だった (past copula). Simple past copula statement
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Very simple past copula. Vocabulary: Very basic (病気). Length: Very short. Context: Past illness
verification.overall_notes: Perfect simple N5 example. Clear and natural

verification.target_vocab_notes: ほしい appears as predicate
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: 何か (something), い-adjective 甘い, もの (thing), が (subject), ほしい. Standard desire pattern
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple ほしい desire. Vocabulary: N5 (何か、甘い、もの). Length: Short. Context: Food desire
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example. Natural expression of wanting something sweet

verification.target_vocab_notes: ほしい appears as predicate
verification.grammar_notes: Uses basic N5: が (subject), ほしい. Simple desire statement
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Very simple desire. Vocabulary: Very basic (飴). Length: Very short. Context: Wanting candy
verification.overall_notes: Perfect simple N5 example. Very clear and basic

verification.target_vocab_notes: 欲しい appears in relative clause modifying もの
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: 欲しいもの (thing wanted), は (topic), 何でも (whatever), あげる (give), よ. The 何でも pattern is acceptable N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Relative clause + 何でも. Vocabulary: N5 (あげる). Length: Medium. Context: Generous offer
verification.overall_notes: Advanced N5 but acceptable. Shows 欲しい in relative clause naturally

verification.target_vocab_notes: ほしい appears as predicate - DUPLICATE of example 0
verification.grammar_notes: Identical to example 0
verification.difficulty_notes: Same as example 0
verification.overall_notes: DUPLICATE: This is identical to example 0. Should be removed or replaced with different example

verification.target_vocab_notes: ほしい appears as predicate - DUPLICATE of example 1
verification.grammar_notes: Identical to example 1
verification.difficulty_notes: Same as example 1
verification.overall_notes: DUPLICATE: This is identical to example 1. Should be removed or replaced

verification.target_vocab_notes: ほしい appears in question
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: が (subject), ほしい, です, か. Simple desire question
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple desire question. Vocabulary: Very basic (車). Length: Very short. Context: Want question
verification.overall_notes: Clean simple N5 question. Natural and clear

verification.target_vocab_notes: 木 appears in 木の下 (under the tree)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: の下 (under), で (location), ちょっと (a bit), 休む, よう (volitional), よ. While よう is volitional, used with よ for casual suggestions it's acceptable N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Volitional suggestion. Vocabulary: N5 (下、休む). Length: Short. Context: Rest suggestion
verification.overall_notes: Acceptable N5. Casual volitional よう+よ for suggestions is standard

verification.target_vocab_notes: 木 appears in 木の下 (under the tree)
verification.grammar_notes: Contains past ている (座っていた - was sitting). Past progressive ていた is N4+ according to guidelines
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Past ていた (N4+). Vocabulary: N5 (下、座る). Length: Short. Context: Past action description
verification.overall_notes: Contains N4+ past ていた form. Should use simpler past tense 座った

verification.target_vocab_notes: 木 appears in 木の下 (under the tree)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: は (topic), の下 (under), に (location), た (past). Simple past tense statement
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple past tense. Vocabulary: N5 (下、座る). Length: Short. Context: Past action
verification.overall_notes: Clean N5 example. Natural use of 木 in locative phrase

verification.target_vocab_notes: CRITICAL: This sentence is about rain (雨が降ってきた). There is NO 木 (tree) in this sentence at all. Clear false positive/data matching error
verification.kana_notes: Kana matches sentence but sentence doesn't contain target vocab
verification.grammar_notes: Contains てきた (降ってきた) which is N4+. Irrelevant since target vocab not present
verification.difficulty_notes: Not applicable - wrong sentence
verification.overall_notes: CRITICAL: Target vocabulary 木 (tree) does NOT appear in this sentence about rain. Data matching error

verification.target_vocab_notes: CRITICAL: This sentence is about bringing water (お水を持ってきて). There is NO 木 (tree) in this sentence. Another false positive
verification.kana_notes: Kana matches but target vocab absent
verification.grammar_notes: Contains てくる (持ってきて) compound. Irrelevant since target vocab not present
verification.difficulty_notes: Not applicable
verification.overall_notes: CRITICAL: Target vocabulary 木 (tree) does NOT appear. False positive - probably matched き in 持ってきて

verification.target_vocab_notes: CRITICAL: This sentence is about wind (風が出てきました). There is NO 木 (tree). Third consecutive false positive
verification.kana_notes: Kana matches but target vocab absent
verification.grammar_notes: Contains てきた (出てきました) which is N4+. Irrelevant
verification.difficulty_notes: Not applicable
verification.overall_notes: CRITICAL: Target vocabulary 木 (tree) does NOT appear. False positive - probably matched き in 出てきました

verification.target_vocab_notes: 薬 appears as object in 薬を飲む
verification.kana_notes: Shows 行けません but in context should likely be いけません. The kanji 行 is wrong - should be plain hiragana いけません for 'must'
verification.grammar_notes: Contains なければいけません (must) which is a conditional obligation pattern. The なければ is conditional form (N4+) and the overall pattern is complex
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: なければいけません obligation (N4+). Vocabulary: N5 (飲む). Length: Medium. Context: Medicine obligation
verification.overall_notes: Contains N4+ なければいけません pattern plus kana error (行けません). Too advanced

verification.target_vocab_notes: 薬 appears in possessive phrase
verification.kana_notes: Shows のみほう but should be のみかた (飲み方). Should be かた not ほう for 'way/method'
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: の (possessive), 飲み方 (way to drink - masu stem + 方), を, 教える, てください. The V-masu + 方 pattern is standard N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: ~方 method pattern + てください. Vocabulary: N5 (飲む、教える). Length: Medium. Context: Medicine instruction request
verification.overall_notes: Grammar is acceptable N5 but has kana error (のみほう→のみかた). Otherwise good example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 薬 appears as object
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: もう (already), を (object), ました (past polite), か (question). Simple past question with もう
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple past question with もう. Vocabulary: Very basic (飲む). Length: Short. Context: Medicine check
verification.overall_notes: Clean N5 example. Natural check-in question about medicine

verification.target_vocab_notes: まずい appears meaning 'bad/inappropriate' (not taste-related)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: で (location), たばこ吸う (smoke), の (nominalizer), は (topic), よ. The nominalization is acceptable N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Nominalization with の. Vocabulary: N5 (学校、たばこ、吸う). Length: Medium. Context: School rule
verification.overall_notes: Acceptable N5. まずい here means 'inappropriate/bad' not 'bad-tasting', which is appropriate usage

verification.target_vocab_notes: まずい appears meaning 'not good/bad-tasting'
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: は (topic), い-adjective 安い, です, が (but), です. Simple contrastive statement with が conjunction
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple contrastive が. Vocabulary: N5 (店、安い). Length: Medium. Context: Restaurant evaluation
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example. Natural use of まずい for bad-tasting food with が contrast

verification.target_vocab_notes: 多い appears as predicate
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: には (topic with は), は (topic), い-adjective 多い, が (but), だ. The contrastive が is acceptable N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Contrastive が + special だ. Vocabulary: N5 but 特別 might be borderline. Length: Long. Context: Relationships
verification.overall_notes: Advanced N5 but acceptable. Shows contrastive use naturally

verification.target_vocab_notes: 多い appears as predicate with んです
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: には (in), が (subject), い-adjective 多い, んです (explanatory), よ. The んです pattern is acceptable N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Explanatory んです. Vocabulary: N5 (冬、雪). Length: Short. Context: Weather description
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example. Natural use of 多い with explanatory んです

verification.target_vocab_notes: 多い appears in past tense 多かった
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: の (possessive), は (topic), が (subject), かった (past i-adjective). Simple past statement
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple past adjective. Vocabulary: N5 (前、冬、雪). Length: Short. Context: Past weather
verification.overall_notes: Clean N5 example. Natural past tense use of 多い

verification.target_vocab_notes: 動物 appears in possessive phrase その動物の名前
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: は (topic), その (that), の (possessive), を (object), 知っている. 知っている is acceptable N5 state verb
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple statement with 知っている. Vocabulary: N5 (名前). Length: Medium. Context: Animal identification
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example. Natural use of 動物 with 知っている

verification.target_vocab_notes: 動物 appears in compound 動物園 (zoo)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5: time expression 昨日, に (direction), 行く, た. Simple past tense statement
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Very simple past tense. Vocabulary: N5 (昨日、行く). Length: Very short. Context: Zoo visit
verification.overall_notes: Perfect simple N5 example. 動物園 is common N5 compound with 動物
